As a certified Kubestronaut, I focus on building secure, reliable, and audit-ready cloud-native systems. I’ve led deployments, built CI/CD pipelines, and automated infrastructure with Terraform, Jenkins, and SaltStack. My approach is hands-on and collaborative, with a passion for mentoring peers and advancing secure cloud-native engineering.

person.name: Max Körbächer
person.bio: Max Körbächer is the Founder and Chief Technology Advisor at Liquid Reply. He is serving as a CNCF Ambassador, a member of the Linux Foundation Europe Advisory Board, and Founder and Emeritus Lead of the former CNCF Environmental Sustainability TAG.His expertise centers on Platform Engineering, which he uses to simplify complex systems in the cloud and AI space, a topic he details in his book, Platform Engineering for Architects. Max is also a dedicated community leader, notably organizing the Cloud Native Days (formerly KCD) in Munich & Ukraine, and the Kubernetes & Cloud Native Meetup in Munich.

person.name: Wolfgang Hofbauer
person.bio: As a Senior Consultant with over a decade of experience in IT infrastructure and Linux, I specialize in the cloud-native landscape, with a primary focus on the Kubernetes ecosystem. I support companies in designing, implementing, and operating modern and secure container platforms. My core strengths include Kubernetes administration, security best practices, and ensuring strict compliance with standards such as BSI IT-Grundschutz and ISO 27001, delivering high quality and strong governance.

person.name: Fabian Kammel
person.bio: I work as a Security Consultant and Engineer where I am dedicated to making the (cloud-native) world a safer place by sharing knowledge, publishing research, and building security tools that are easy to use correctly. I love breaking and fixing CI pipelines, tinkering with software attestations, and automating the boring (security) work. Beyond my recent work on Post-Quantum Cryptography for the Kubernetes blog, I’ve had the pleasure of sharing my insights at KubeCon and SigstoreCon, and as a guest on the Kubernetes Podcast discussing Confidential Computing.

person.name: Gerrit Tamboer
person.bio: As a Platform Engineer with over 15 years of experience, I specialize in Cloud Native technologies and Kubernetes, focusing on driving these innovations to the edge. In 2016, I founded Fullstaq, a consulting company dedicated to advancing Cloud Native solutions, and through its merger into TrueFullstaq, I now serve as Chief Evangelist. I’m passionate about sharing insights and best practices in the CNCF ecosystem, actively engaging in the community to promote Kubernetes and edge computing.

person.name: Seymur Aliyev
person.bio: I am a dedicated and passionate about continuous learning and growth, I stay abreast of the latest developments in DevOps tools and methodologies, constantly seeking opportunities to enhance my skills and contribute to innovative projects. Whether it's containerization with Docker and Kubernetes, configuration management with Ansible, or infrastructure as code with Terraform, I'm always eager to explore new technologies and apply them to real-world scenarios. I'm deeply committed to fostering collaboration and driving positive change within organizations. Whether it's working closely with cross-functional teams to streamline processes, mentoring junior colleagues, or championing a culture of automation and innovation, I believe in the power of teamwork to achieve shared goals and drive success.

person.name: Sun Hyung Choi
person.bio: Technical consultant with a passion for Linux and modern DevOps approaches.

person.name: Valentin Thomas
person.bio: I'm a cloud-native enthusiast with a passion for Kubernetes and building systems that are both secure and reliable. I work with DevSecOps practices, integrating security at every stage of the development lifecycle through zero-trust principles and proactive threat monitoring in containerized environments.  I believe in continuous learning and love the challenge of solving complex problems through collaboration. I think the best solutions come from working together, and the open-source community motivates me every day. I'm looking forward to contributing back and sharing the knowledge I gain along the way.

person.name: Robert Sirchia
person.bio: I create content around CNCF projects, building enablement content around CNCF projects. I help contribute around marketing and community efforts with CNCF projects. I am a new code contributor to a CNCF project

person.name: Ahmed Gaber
person.bio: I’m a cloud native platform engineer and architect based in the UK, focused on building secure, scalable, multi-tenant enterprise Kubernetes platforms. I specialise in platform engineering and Internal Developer Platforms (IDPs), with an emphasis on GitOps (Argo CD), policy-as-code (Kyverno), and software supply-chain security. I share practical lessons, best practices, and proven patterns through mentoring and training engineering teams.

person.name: Savitha Raghunathan
person.bio: Savitha Raghunathan is a Senior Software Engineer at Red Hat, currently focusing on Application Modernization and Cloud Native adoption. Her role as a maintainer for the CNCF Sandbox project Konveyor has provided her with valuable insights into the growing demand for modernizing critical applications to align with cloud-native technologies, thereby deepening her understanding of the application lifecycle. In her previous position, Savitha worked as a platform engineer, where she dedicated herself to building internal developer platforms using Kubernetes and other cloud native projects.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Contributing to CNCF projects has been incredibly fulfilling, and the support and positivity I've experienced from the community during difficult periods, including the pandemic, have deepened my bond with this community. This role as a CNCF Ambassador has given me a chance to reciprocate that support, share my valuable experiences, and facilitate the onboarding of new contributors and adopters into the CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Chaimae Rakhim
person.bio: I'm Chaimae Rakhim, Senior DevOps Engineer with 6 years of experience exploring the cloud-native world. I enjoy working with Kubernetes and multi-cloud platforms across AWS, Azure, and GCP, and believe in learning by doing and growing together with the community.

person.name: Debaprita Majumdar
person.bio: I’m a Senior Cloud Engineer with expertise in designing and managing secure, scalable infrastructures on Azure and GCP, with a strong focus on Kubernetes, GitOps, and modern DevOps practices. I take a holistic approach to cloud engineering, combining integration technologies and solutions architecture to build cloud-native platforms that align with business objectives. With hands-on experience in both the retail and healthcare sectors, I deliver solutions that meet high standards for performance, compliance, and reliability. My work emphasizes automation, observability, and infrastructure as code, enabling fast, consistent deployments and supporting long-term innovation through cloud modernization.

person.name: Mykhailo Pasiechnyk
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer with strong hands-on experience in AWS, Kubernetes, and Infrastructure as Code. I specialize in designing and automating cloud-native platforms using Terraform, EKS, and CNCF technologies, with a focus on security, scalability, and reliability.

person.name: Roberto Fernande Da Silva
person.bio: Roberto is a Solutions Architect at AWS, with a strong focus on the financial sector. His journey with Kubernetes started back in 2018, during his first KubeCon in Seattle — an experience that marked the beginning of a deep, ongoing relationship with cloud-native technologies.  Since then, he’s been helping clients across the maturity spectrum: from those launching their very first cluster, to enterprises managing hundreds of clusters who are scaling governance, resilience, and innovation through platform engineering.  After hard work, the reward is not just rest — it's growth. That mindset drives Roberto: continuously learning, sharing knowledge, and helping teams architect systems that are not only scalable, but sustainable.  Whether building from scratch or evolving mature platforms, he’s passionate about turning complexity into clarity — and creating a Kubernetes-powered future, one cluster at a time.

person.name: Claudio Domingos
person.bio: With over 15 years in IT, I specialize in aligning architecture, operations, and security with industry best practices. My focus is on automation, cloud infrastructure, and DevSecOps to help clients build secure, efficient, and reliable systems. I value clear communication, practical solutions, and continuous improvement. Always open to sharing ideas and learning from others — let’s connect!

person.name: Edson Ribeiro Junior
person.bio: I’m an IT professional originally from Brazil with 17 years of experience, starting my career as a SysAdmin before transitioning into cloud computing in 2014, focusing on AWS and open-source technologies. Over the years, I’ve specialized in containerization and Kubernetes, building deep expertise in CNCF ecosystem tools. I’ve contributed to and supported open-source projects, and I’m passionate about observability, particularly with technologies like Prometheus, Thanos, and OpenTelemetry. Within my organization, I run internal meetups to promote and share knowledge around CNCF technologies and best practices.

person.name: Ankur Gupta
person.bio: With over 15 years of experience in IT, I began my journey in Linux OS customization and RPM packaging before moving into private cloud technologies like OpenStack and VMware. For the past 8 years, I've specialized in Kubernetes, working extensively with CNCF technologies across enterprise platforms such as Red Hat OpenShift and SUSE Rancher. I currently lead the Container Platform team at HPE, supporting global customers with Kubernetes, DevOps, and cloud-native solutions. To expand my capabilities in AI/ML-native workloads on Kubernetes, I recently completed my Master's in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning from Liverpool John Moores University (LJMU), UK.
